rulings.Case Title: LAPASIA COLBERT VS. BIJOU, LLC ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO QUASH SERVICE OF SUMMONS OR STAY OR DISMISS
rulings.Rulings: On the Law and Motion Calendar for December 13, 2024, line 1. DEFENDANT JOSEPH CAROUBA'S MOTION TO QUASH SERVICE OF SUMMONS OR STAY OR DISMISS. Defendant Joseph Carouba's motion to quash service is GRANTED. Carouba's request for judicial notice, which is unopposed, is GRANTED. The entire tentative ruling has been emailed to counsel. For the 9:30 a.m. Law & Motion calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept301tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept301tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(301/JMQ)

Plaintiffs' opposition/response to demurrer must be filed and served no later than January 22, 2025, and Defendant's reply must be filed and served no later than January 28, 2025. rulings.Case Title: ELIZABETH HSU VS. THE REGENTS OF THE UNIVERSITY OF CALIFORNIA ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO STRIKE Amended COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: On the Law and Motion Calendar for December 13, 2024, line 9. DEFENDANT THE REGENTS OF THE UNIVERSITY OF CALIFORNIA'S MOTION TO STRIKE 2ND Amended COMPLAINT. Defendant's unopposed motion to strike is GRANTED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND. Defendant is a public entity, and plaintiff cannot recover punitive damages against it as a matter of law. (Government Code section 818 ["Notwithstanding any other provision of law, a public entity is not liable for damages awarded under Section 3294 of the Civil Code or other damages imposed primarily for the sake of example and by way of punishing the defendant."].) The prevailing party must provide a separate proposed order for each demurrer/motion. For the 9:30 a.m. Law & Motion calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept301tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. rulings.Case Title: ELIZABETH HSU VS. THE REGENTS OF THE UNIVERSITY OF CALIFORNIA ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER to Amended COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: On the Law and Motion Calendar for December 13, 2024, line 9. DEFENDANT THE REGENTS OF THE UNIVERSITY OF CALIFORNIA'S DEMURRER to 2ND Amended COMPLAINT. The demurrer to the second amended complaint is SUSTAINED WITH 15 DAYS LEAVE TO AMEND. Exhaustion of administrative remedies is a "jurisdictional prerequisite to resort to the courts." (Ortega v. Contra Costa Community College Dist. (2007) 156 Cal.App.4th 1073, 1081.) Under FEHA, a party must file a timely administrative complaint with the CRD and obtain a "right-to-sue" notice. (Government Code sections 12960, 12965(c).) The failure to file an administrative charge before commencing suit is ground for dismissal of the FEHA claim. (Martin v. Lockheed Missiles & Space Co., Inc. (1994) 29 Cal.App.4th 1718, 1724.) Plaintiff must plead compliance with the administrative requirements. (Hood v. Hacienda La Puente Unified Sch. Dist. (1998) 65 Cal.App.4th 435, 439 ["Plaintiff had a duty to plead exhaustion of administrative remedies or facts which indicate the duty to do so has been excused."]; Williams v. Housing Authority of Los Angeles (2004) 121 Cal.App.4th 708, 736 [demurrer sustained where Plaintiff's complaint failed to allege satisfaction of administrative exhaustion requirement].) Here, plaintiff fails to plead facts that could support a finding she exhausted her administrative remedies. Additionally, as to the First, Second and Third causes of action, the second amended complaint fails to plead facts sufficient to state FEHA claims. Plaintiff must, for example, identify her disability, describe substantially limited life activities, identify accommodation requests and related activities and identify facts sufficient to support inferences of age and race discrimination. Plaintiff is granted 15 days leave to amend. For the 9:30 a.m. Law & Motion calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept301tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept301tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(301/JMQ)

Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling (as directed above) and must email it to contestdept301tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Discovery Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(301/JQM).

rulings.Case Title: DOLORES STREET COMMUNITY SERVICES, INC., VS. MARTY'S PLACE AFFORDABLE HOUSING CORPORATION ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Deem Requests For Admission, Set One Admitted And For Monetary Sanctions Against Defendant
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Discovery calendar for Friday, December 13, 2024, Line 8. 1 - PLAINTIFF DOLORES STREET COMMUNITY SERVICES, INC.'s Motion To Deem Requests For Admission, Set One Admitted And For Monetary Sanctions Against Defendant. Plaintiff Dolores Street Community Services Inc.'s discovery motions are resolved as follows (a separate proposed order must be lodged for each motion): Plaintiff's Motion To Compel Defendant's Response to Requests For Production, Set One, And For Monetary Sanctions Against Defendant, which is unopposed, is GRANTED. Defendant Marty's Place Affordable Housing Corporation's response-or non-response-to the requests is not substantially justified. The court orders Defendant to pay Plaintiff $800 as sanctions. rulings.Case Title: DANIEL B. POWELL VS. SAN FRANCISCO FIRE DEPARTMENT ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO STRIKE DEMURRER to Amended COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: On the Law and Motion Calendar for December 13, 2024, line 10. PLAINTIFF DANIEL POWELL'S MOTION TO STRIKE DEMURRER to 1ST Amended COMPLAINT. Plaintiff Daniel Powell's Motion to Strike Demurrer to First Amended Complaint is DENIED because the motion was filed less than 16 days before the hearing in violation of CCP 1005. Additionally, the motion is denied as moot, given the court has already ruled on the demurrer that Plaintiff seeks to strike from the record. For the 9:30 a.m. Law & Motion calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept301tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept301tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(301/JMQ)
